nodes.content: Three months later, manufacturers announced layoffs, bank credit standards tightened, and new orders fell again despite the central bank staying hawkish.
nodes.label: macroeconomy signal T1

classification.rationale: The signal indicates declining economic activity with layoffs and falling orders, but lacks specific data on magnitude or duration.

nodes.content: The economy shows clear signs of weakening as manufacturers announce layoffs, bank credit standards tighten, and new orders decline further despite the central bank maintaining a hawkish stance. This combination suggests a demand-side contraction is taking hold, with business activity deteriorating and financial conditions becoming more restrictive.
nodes.label: macroeconomy inference T1

key_mechanisms: Manufacturer layoffs reduce household income and consumer spending, creating negative feedback loops
Tightening bank credit standards restricts business investment and working capital, amplifying the downturn
Falling new orders indicate weakening business confidence and forward-looking demand
Hawkish monetary policy continues to exert pressure despite emerging economic weakness
The interaction of these factors increases the likelihood of a self-reinforcing economic contraction
